items.title: Belgium player left the World Cup for the birth of his 1st child, and people had opinions
items.summary_auto: Belgium winger Jérémy Doku flew from the World Cup to be with his wife for the birth of their son on Sunday, sparking a debate about professional athletes and fatherhood.

items.title: Jérémy Doku left the World Cup for the birth of his 1st child, and people had opinions
items.summary_auto: Belgium winger Jérémy Doku flew from the World Cup to be with his wife for the birth of their son on Sunday, sparking a debate about professional athletes and fatherhood.

items.title: Ronaldo makes World Cup history with 2-goal effort in Portugal's 5-0 win against Uzbekistan
items.summary_auto: Cristiano Ronaldo scored two goals to help Portugal roll to a 5-0 win over Uzbekistan at the World Cup on Tuesday in Houston, bouncing back after a disappointing performance in the team's opening match.

items.title: Quebec military man pleads guilty to 7 charges after arrests tied to alleged violent extremism
items.summary_auto: A Quebec man has pleaded guilty to seven out of 11 charges related to firearms possession and controlled materials after his 2025 arrest, when he was an active member of the military. He was arrested by the RCMP during an investigation where three other men were accused of facilitating terrorism.

items.title: Lytton, B.C., man describes harrowing evacuation before Saw Creek fire destroyed his home
items.summary_auto: The Saw Creek fire, discovered on June 19, continues to burn out of control just south of Lytton — the village in B.C.’s Interior that was destroyed by wildfire around this time five years ago.

items.title: 6 schools evacuated in northern Manitoba city due to bomb threats
items.summary_auto: Nearly every school in the northern Manitoba city of Thompson was evacuated Tuesday after a series of bomb threats.

items.title: Brady Tkachuk says he asked for a trade from Ottawa Senators to start the 'next chapter' of his career
items.summary_auto: Former Ottawa Senators captain Brady Tkachuk said during a media tour on Tuesday that he made a very hard decision to move on to the next chapter of his life and career when he requested a trade from the team that drafted him.

items.title: Kingston, Ont., officials cheer Alto high-speed rail announcement, but concerns remain
items.summary_auto: Officials in Kingston, Ont., welcomed news that the federal government prefers a southern route for high-speed rail, with a potential stop in their city. That change would spare property owners along the northern route, but concerns linger.

items.title: Toronto siblings win 'life-changing' $75M in Lotto Max jackpot
items.summary_auto: Two Toronto siblings who came to Canada with dreams of a better life have won $75 million in a Lotto Max draw, the Ontario Lottery and Gaming Commission has announced.

items.title: Shooter, 7 others sentenced to decades in prison after violent incident at Texas ICE facility
items.summary_auto: A man who shot and wounded a police officer during a protest outside a Texas immigration centre last year was sentenced to 100 years in federal prison Tuesday, while other protesters accused of having links to antifa were given multiple decades in federal prison.

items.title: Actress Christina Ricci shares post condemning Tonight Show interview of fighter Conor McGregor
items.summary_auto: Actress Christina Ricci shared a post criticizing late-night host Jimmy Fallon after the latter interviewed mixed martial artist Conor McGregor on his show. In 2024, an Irish civil court jury found McGregor liable for sexual assault at a 2018 Dublin party.

items.title: American kayaker drowns in Okanagan Lake: RCMP
items.summary_auto: Police are investigating the fatal drowning of an American kayaker in B.C. 's Okanagan Lake on Friday evening. RCMP say they are still trying to notify the person's next of kin.

items.title: 'Deep concern': Windsor lawmaker urges province to examine new Moroun concrete business
items.summary_auto: A provincial legislator is urging the Ontario government to "closely examine" a new concrete operation in Canada backed by the wealthy Moroun family of Michigan, citing "deep concern" over potential air pollution and health effects on nearby residents.

items.title: Leylah Fernandez's Wimbledon tune-up ends with Round of 16 loss in Germany
items.summary_auto: Canadian Leylah Fernandez's run at the Bad Homburg Open in Germany ended Tuesday with a 6-3, 6-4 loss to China's Wang Xinyu in the Round of 16.

items.title: Edmonton Oilers hire Mike Babcock as new head coach after NHL wraps conduct investigation
items.summary_auto: Babcock signed a two-year, $8 million US contract with the Blue Jackets on July 1, 2023, but resigned just before the start of training camp amid an investigation into reports invading the privacy of his players.

items.title: Byelection clock ticking as MPs officially resign. How does it impact Liberals' majority?
Why some in B.C. aren't cheering on the provincial and federal 'bailout' of the housing sector
items.summary_auto: A week after the federal and provincial governments announced new subsidies for the development sector, mayors in British Columbia say the details are as empty as the province's thousands of unsold condo units.

items.title: Why local B.C. governments aren't cheering on the provincial and federal 'bailout' of the housing sector
items.summary_auto: A week after the federal and provincial governments announced new subsidies for the development sector, mayors in British Columbia say the details are as empty as the province's thousands of unsold condo units.

items.title: Tech stocks drop, pulling world shares down
items.summary_auto: Major North American indexes were down midday on Tuesday, after Asian and European markets also slipped. Technology stocks, which have surged amid the AI frenzy, were the biggest drags on the market.

items.title: Israeli gunfire kills 2 in south Lebanon, testing Iran-linked ceasefire
items.summary_auto: Israeli gunfire killed two people in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, Lebanon's Civil Defence and health ministry said, prompting Iran-backed Hezbollah to accuse Israel of violating a ceasefire that has largely held since Sunday.

items.title: Frank talk on Ottawa police misconduct dominates 1st in-person board meeting in years
items.summary_auto: Gender-based misconduct and what to do about it dominated the discussion at the first in-person Ottawa police oversight board meeting in more than three years Monday night.

items.title: Surrey, B.C., extortionist told police that Bishnoi contractor paid $3K for fiery attack
items.summary_auto: Three Indian nationals jailed earlier this month for their roles in a fiery attack on a South Surrey home in February were paid $1,000 each by a subcontractor for the Bishnoi gang, according to a record of their sentencing.

items.title: 1st bison calves born at Batoche in nearly 150 years get Michif names
items.summary_auto: Groos tet (Big Head) and Chiraan (Northern Light) are some of the Michif names that students helped choose for 16 bison calves born at Batoche this spring.

items.title: Drive-thru chatbots are clocking in at fast-food chains
France sees its hottest day on record amid early heat wave in Europe
items.summary_auto: Millions of people across Europe were exposed to extreme and exceptionally high temperatures on Tuesday, with 40 fatalities from drowning recorded in France in the past week as residents seek relief from the searing heat.

items.title: Is the U.K. ungovernable?
items.summary_auto: With Keir Starmer out, the U.K.’s had six different Prime Ministers resign in the last 10 years. Why? And what does it say about what it takes to govern the country?

items.title: Years without fluoridated water in N.B. show pattern of tooth decay experts warned about
items.summary_auto: After more than a decade without fluoridated water in Saint John, the dental community is seeing a surge in tooth decay among young people.

items.title: N.S. banned cellphones in classrooms 2 years ago. Here's how it's going
items.summary_auto: Nearly two years into its provincewide ban on cellphones in Nova Scotia public schools, the province's education minister says it's helping to reduce distraction and improve learning. Some students are following the rules, while others need more convincing.

items.title: Years without fluoridated water show pattern of tooth decay experts warned about
items.summary_auto: After more than a decade without fluoridated water in Saint John, the dental community is seeing a surge in tooth decay among young people.

items.title: Why the wait? Delays in disclosure of evidence repeatedly flagged in N.L. Jordan cases
items.summary_auto: An analysis of cases impacted by R. v. Jordan shows that one of the driving factors is the delay in handing over disclosure — evidence in the case — to the defence.

items.title: Are more police officers getting killed in the line of duty? Here's what the data tells us
items.summary_auto: An armed standoff at a hotel in Côte-des-Neiges left a police officer, a civilian and a suspect dead. The shooting marked the third time an officer in Canada was killed in less than two weeks, raising questions about whether violence against officers is increasing.

items.title: Hamilton teen receives Carnegie Medal for pulling man from tent fire
items.summary_auto: A Hamilton teen who pulled a man from a tent engulfed in flames last year has been awarded the Carnegie Medal, given to those who "risked serious injury or death attempting to save others in acts of extraordinary heroism." Zeke Fox was 17 when he dragged Shawn Goodwin out of the flames that destroyed his encampment at Bayfront Park in January 2025.

items.title: Ontario's freedom of information changes are law. Why experts say they could hurt the government
items.summary_auto: The reality of Premier Doug Ford’s changes to Ontario’s freedom of information system are beginning to take hold as rejections and denials make their way to requesters.

items.title: How the FIFA World Cup is bringing a pan-African vibe to life in Toronto
items.summary_auto: Three of Toronto's five World Cup 2026 group games feature African teams. It's giving African fans in the city a "once-in-a-lifetime" opportunity to watch some of their continent's top talents and vibe with the best elements of pan-African culture.

items.title: Teen charged with attempted murder of 8-year-old girl in Quadeville, Ont., to stand trial in 2027
items.summary_auto: The teenager charged last summer with the attempted murder of an eight-year-old girl in Quadeville, Ont., is scheduled to stand trial in early 2027.

items.title: Do you want AI with that? Chatbots could take your next order at the drive-thru
items.summary_auto: Across the United States, fast food chains are turning to AI to run their drive-thru counters - and Canada could be next. Companies say the technology now has higher accuracy than human employees - and some labour organizers worry it could displace fast food workers.

items.title: Ransom note sent days after Nancy Guthrie's abduction said she was dead, sources say
items.summary_auto: A ransom note sent days after the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, the mother of NBC Today show host Savannah Guthrie, said the 84-year-old had died, CNN and other news organizations reported Monday, citing law enforcement sources.

items.title: Ford government denies editing Ford Fest photo, says it corrected 'orange hue'
items.summary_auto: The Ontario government’s Official Opposition slammed Premier Doug Ford Monday after a photograph shared online by two PC MPPs showcasing Ford Fest — an annual community event held by the party — appeared to be edited to make protesters look like supporters.

items.title: Did the Ontario Progressive Conservatives edit a Ford Fest photo? Here's what we found
items.summary_auto: The Ontario government’s Official Opposition slammed Premier Doug Ford Monday after a photograph shared online by two PC MPPs showcasing Ford Fest — an annual community event held by the party — appeared to be edited to make protesters look like supporters.
